description_long: This guard rail consists of a continuous, sinuous metal tube bent into a repeating wave or serpentine pattern, supported by four vertical posts at each end and at two intermediate points. The rail is constructed from a round, hollow steel tube, giving it both strength and a smooth, industrial appearance. The posts are capped with circular base plates, likely for bolting to a hard surface such as concrete or asphalt. The entire structure is coated in a dark, matte finish, which is typical for outdoor urban infrastructure to resist weathering and corrosion. There are no visible joints or separable parts, indicating a single welded or bent piece. The ends of the rail are blunt and terminate vertically into the ground, with no additional safety or decorative treatments.
description: A wavy, tubular metal guard rail with four vertical posts and a dark matte finish, designed for urban outdoor use. The rail features a continuous serpentine shape and blunt vertical ends.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full serpentine wave pattern and all four vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the rail, with the wave pattern compressed into a single line.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, displaying the wavy rail and post arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the posts.
